shortAnswer: Chloride (Cl) is an electrolyte that helps regulate acid-base balance and fluid balance. Deviations from normal can indicate kidney or metabolic problems. It is usually assessed alongside sodium and potassium. Discuss any abnormal values with your doctor.
metaDescription: Chloride elevated or low? Normal ranges, causes, and meaning of this electrolyte: explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Does my chloride level indicate an acid-base problem?
Should I have my other electrolytes checked at the same time?
Could a medication be affecting my chloride level?
